identity.short_name: Unit-circle root count -> coefficient autocorrelation via Parseval viewpoint

problem_spec.summary: Composite (CHAIN) family: compute bridge quantity T from component A (Unit-circle roots of (z+1)^6  (z^3+1)^2); then use that T as a required parameter in component B (Coefficient lag autocorrelation via Fourier coefficient and cosine moments) to determine the final output (reduced mod 1000). Instance summary: Count unit-circle solutions of a binomial square-difference equation to obtain T, then use T as the exponent in a trinomial-power coefficient autocorrelation computation, reduced mod 1000.

parameter(s).name: equation_structure
parameter(s).purpose: forces a fixed unit-circle solution count T

parameter(s).name: n
parameter(s).purpose: Defines the family P_n(z)=(z+1)^{2n}-(z^n+1)^2; the instance uses n=3 (so degree 6 vs squared degree 3).

parameter(s).name: exponent
parameter(s).purpose: controls the power on each quadratic factor, hence the degree and sum length

parameter(s).name: linear_coefficient_u
parameter(s).purpose: sets the conjugate-like quadratic pair (x^2+u x+1),(x^2-u x+1) enabling cosine reduction

parameter(s).name: lag_t
parameter(s).purpose: specifies which coefficient correlation Σ a_k a_{k+t} is extracted as a Fourier coefficient

parameter(s).name: T_bridge
parameter(s).domain: derived integer computed from component A invariants
parameter(s).purpose: interface quantity produced by component A and consumed by component B (non-stapled coupling).

invariant(s).name: unit_circle_modulus_for_conjugate_reciprocal_pair
invariant(s).statement: If a quadratic has real coefficients and root product 1, then its nonreal roots are conjugate and lie on |z|=1.

invariant(s).name: unit_circle_phase_alignment
invariant(s).statement: For z=e^{iθ}, (z+1)^{2n} and (z^n+1)^2 both carry the phase factor e^{inθ}, so P_n(e^{iθ})=0 reduces to a purely real equation 2^{2n-2} cos^{2n}(θ/2) = cos^2(nθ/2).

invariant(s).name: triple_angle_closure_for_n3
invariant(s).statement: When n=3, the reduced equation involves cos(3x), allowing elimination via cos(3x)=4cos^3 x - 3cos x to obtain a polynomial equation in cos x.

invariant(s).name: correlation_is_fourier_coefficient
invariant(s).statement: If P(x)= a_k x^k, then _k a_k a_{k+t} = [x^t] P(x)P(x^{-1}) = (1/2)_0^{2} |P(e^{i})|^2 e^{-it} d. For real coefficients, this equals (1/2) |P(e^{i})|^2 cos(t) d.

invariant(s).name: bridge_interface_invariant
invariant(s).statement: The composite is coupled by the interface variable T: component A uniquely determines T, and component B is defined/solved only after substituting this specific T into its parameter slot.

essential_assumption(s): Distinct unit-circle solutions are counted without multiplicity.
z lies on the unit circle so z=e^{iθ} with θ real.
Cancelling the common factor e^{i3θ} is valid since it is never zero.
